class.name: OTelSpan
class.brief_description: A single operation within a distributed trace.
class.description: Represents one OpenTelemetry span: a named, timed operation identified by a trace ID and span ID, optionally parented to another span. Carries attributes, events, links, and a status, and serializes to the OTLP wire format.

method.name: add_attribute
method.description: Adds or replaces attribute [param key] with [param value].

method.name: add_event
method.description: Records a timestamped event named [param name] with optional [param attributes]. If [param timestamp] is [code]0[/code], the current time is used.

method.name: add_link
method.description: Adds a link to another span identified by [param trace_id] and [param span_id], with optional [param attributes].

method.name: generate_span_id
method.description: Returns a new random 16-hex-character (64-bit) span ID.

method.name: generate_trace_id
method.description: Returns a new random 32-hex-character (128-bit) trace ID.

method.name: is_ended
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the span has been ended with [method mark_ended].

method.name: is_valid_span_id
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if [param id] is a valid 16-hex-character (64-bit) span ID.

method.name: is_valid_trace_id
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if [param id] is a valid 32-hex-character (128-bit) trace ID.

method.name: mark_ended
method.description: Marks the span as ended. See [method is_ended].

method.name: to_otlp_dict
method.description: Returns the span as an OTLP-format Dictionary.

members.member: Key-value attributes attached to the span.
Span end time, in nanoseconds since the Unix epoch.
Timestamped events recorded during the span. See [method add_event].
Links to other spans. See [method add_link].
Span ID of the parent span, or empty for a root span.
Span start time, in nanoseconds since the Unix epoch.
The 32-hex-character (128-bit) trace ID this span belongs to.

constants.constant: Server-side handling of a remote request.
Client-side request to a remote service.
Producer sending a message to a broker.
Consumer receiving a message from a broker.
